explicacion: A negative pressure relief valve protects the aircraft structure if outside pressure becomes greater than cabin pressure.

pregunta: The four basic components of a vapor-cycle air conditioning system are:
explicacion: The basic vapor-cycle system consists of a compressor, condenser, expansion valve, and evaporator.

pregunta: When safetying a turnbuckle, what is the maximum number of exposed threads permitted?
explicacion: A properly adjusted turnbuckle should have no more than three exposed threads before being safetied.

pregunta: When safetying a turnbuckle barrel, the safety wire must have a minimum of:
explicacion: Standard turnbuckle safety requires a minimum of four wraps of safety wire.

pregunta: Rivet holes are normally drilled approximately:
opciones: Exactly the same diameter as the rivet
explicacion: Standard practice is to drill rivet holes approximately 0.003 inch larger than the nominal rivet diameter.

pregunta: When joining sheets of unequal thickness, the manufactured head of a solid rivet should normally be placed on the:
explicacion: The manufactured head is normally placed against the thinner material to reduce the chance of damaging it during riveting.

pregunta: After heat treatment, 2017 (17S) aluminum alloy rivets should normally be driven within:
explicacion: 2017 (17S) rivets must be installed within approximately 30 minutes after removal from refrigeration following heat treatment.

pregunta: Which aircraft rivet alloy requires heat treatment before installation?
explicacion: 2017 aluminum alloy rivets require heat treatment and refrigeration before use.

pregunta: Cracks around the head of a driven rivet are usually caused by:
explicacion: Driving a rivet excessively work-hardens the material and can cause cracks to form around the head.

pregunta: The shop head of a properly driven solid rivet should be approximately:
opciones: One rivet diameter wide and 0.25D high
1.5 times the rivet diameter wide and 0.5D high
Two rivet diameters wide and one diameter high
The same size as the manufactured head
explicacion: A properly formed shop head is approximately 1.5 times the rivet diameter in width and one-half the rivet diameter in height.

pregunta: Which aircraft structural inspection method uses fluorescent dye viewed under ultraviolet light?
explicacion: Zyglo is a fluorescent penetrant inspection method that uses ultraviolet light to reveal surface defects.

pregunta: Fiber self-locking nuts should generally not be installed where the temperature exceeds:
explicacion: The nylon insert in a fiber self-locking nut loses its locking ability at temperatures above approximately 250°F (121°C).

pregunta: Aircraft electrical junction boxes should normally be installed with the open side:
explicacion: Junction boxes are installed with the open side down to prevent the accumulation of moisture and foreign material.

pregunta: When routing aircraft electrical wiring, it should normally be installed:
opciones: Above flammable fluid lines whenever possible
explicacion: Electrical wiring should be routed above fuel, oil, and hydraulic lines whenever practical to reduce the risk of contamination in the event of a leak.

pregunta: Before making an electrical bonding connection, the mating surfaces should be:
explicacion: Good electrical bonding requires clean metal-to-metal contact. Paint, primer, oxidation, and corrosion must be removed from the bonding surfaces.

pregunta: What is the primary purpose of a reverse current relay in a DC generator system?
opciones: Prevent battery current from flowing back through the generator
explicacion: A reverse current relay disconnects the generator when its output drops below battery voltage, preventing the battery from motoring the generator.

pregunta: The primary purpose of a split-bus electrical system is to:
opciones: Separate electrical loads into independent buses
explicacion: A split-bus system separates essential and non-essential electrical loads, improving reliability and fault isolation.

pregunta: How should a wooden propeller be stored when not installed on an aircraft?
opciones: Vertically in a dry, temperature-controlled area
explicacion: Wooden propellers should be stored vertically in a dry, controlled environment to prevent moisture damage and warping.

pregunta: What is the purpose of tracking a propeller?
opciones: Ensure all blades rotate in the same plane
explicacion: Propeller tracking verifies that all blade tips rotate in the same plane, minimizing vibration.

pregunta: When operating a constant-speed propeller in very cold weather before shutdown, the propeller should normally be left in:
explicacion: Many manufacturers recommend leaving a constant-speed propeller in the high-pitch position before shutdown in very cold weather to help prevent moisture accumulation in the hub. Always follow the aircraft maintenance manual.

pregunta: The specific gravity of the electrolyte cannot be used to determine the state of charge of which battery type?
explicacion: The electrolyte specific gravity of a nickel-cadmium battery remains essentially constant regardless of its state of charge.

pregunta: Aircraft fluid lines are color-coded primarily to:
opciones: Identify the type of fluid carried
explicacion: Aircraft fluid lines are identified by color codes or markings to indicate the fluid carried, simplifying maintenance and reducing the possibility of incorrect servicing.

pregunta: A stall warning system normally activates approximately how many knots before the actual stall?
explicacion: Most stall warning systems are designed to activate approximately 5 to 10 knots before the aircraft reaches the stall speed.

pregunta: A capacitance-type fuel quantity indicating system normally operates on:
explicacion: Many aircraft capacitance fuel quantity systems use a 115-volt AC excitation source.

pregunta: A capacitance-type fuel quantity system primarily measures:
opciones: Fuel capacitance, which is converted into fuel quantity
explicacion: The sensing element measures changes in capacitance caused by the dielectric properties of the fuel. Depending on the aircraft, the indication may be displayed as fuel quantity or converted to weight.

pregunta: Drain grommets should normally be installed after applying which coat of dope?
explicacion: Drain grommets are typically installed after the second coat of dope during fabric covering.

pregunta: Where is the oil temperature probe normally located?
explicacion: The oil temperature bulb or probe is commonly installed in the oil line entering the engine to measure oil inlet temperature.

pregunta: What is a gascolator?
opciones: A glass sediment bowl and main fuel strainer used on many small aircraft
explicacion: A gascolator is the main fuel strainer on many light aircraft. It removes water and sediment before fuel reaches the engine.

pregunta: Aircraft tires used at speeds of approximately 160 mph (257 km/h) or less are generally classified as:
explicacion: Some aircraft tire classifications identify Type III tires for operation up to approximately 160 mph. Always verify with the manufacturer's specifications.

pregunta: Most aircraft anti-skid systems are automatically deactivated below approximately:
explicacion: Many anti-skid systems are disabled below approximately 15 to 20 mph to allow the aircraft to come to a complete stop. The exact value varies by aircraft manufacturer.

pregunta: If aircraft brakes grab or jerk during application, the most likely cause is:
opciones: Low nitrogen pressure in the strut
explicacion: Brake grabbing or jerking is commonly caused by oil contamination, worn brake linings, warped discs, or damaged brake drums.

pregunta: Typical micron ratings for aircraft filters are:
opciones: Hydraulic paper: 3 µm, Metal: 5 µm, Fuel: 40 µm, Pneumatic: 10 µm
Hydraulic paper: 10 µm, Metal: 25 µm, Fuel: 100 µm, Pneumatic: 40 µm
All aircraft filters are rated at 10 µm
All aircraft filters are rated at 40 µm
explicacion: These are common training values found in some study guides. Actual filter micron ratings vary depending on the aircraft manufacturer and system design.

pregunta: What component reduces the high pressure from an oxygen cylinder to a usable pressure for the flight crew?
explicacion: In aircraft oxygen systems, a pressure-reducing regulator lowers cylinder pressure to a usable pressure. Diluter-demand systems incorporate a diluter-demand oxygen regulator.

pregunta: How can a carbon-fouled spark plug typically be identified?
opciones: Light gray deposits on the electrodes
Black, dry, sooty deposits on the firing end
explicacion: Carbon fouling is identified by black, dry, sooty deposits on the spark plug firing end. Oil fouling usually appears wet and oily.

pregunta: What is the maximum number of splices normally permitted in a single wooden spar?
explicacion: Standard wood aircraft repair practices generally permit no more than two splices in any one spar.

pregunta: Hydraulic system chatter is most commonly caused by:
opciones: Air entering the hydraulic pump inlet or suction line
explicacion: Hydraulic chatter (cavitation) is commonly caused by air entering the pump suction line or inlet.

pregunta: During manufacture of an aircraft control cable, the proof load applied is approximately:
opciones: 30% of the cable breaking strength for 1 minute
50% of the cable breaking strength for 2 minutes
60% of the cable breaking strength for 3 minutes
100% of the cable breaking strength for 30 seconds
explicacion: Aircraft control cables are proof-loaded to approximately 60% of their rated breaking strength for about 3 minutes during manufacture.

pregunta: How is a float normally checked for leaks during maintenance?
opciones: Immerse it in water for approximately 24 hours
Heat it with a heat gun
Fill it with fuel and inspect immediately
explicacion: A float may be immersed in water for approximately 24 hours to determine if leakage occurs.

pregunta: The maximum allowable free play at the trailing edge of a trim tab is generally:
opciones: 2% of tab span if the tab is less than 35% of the control surface span, and 1% if greater than 35%
explicacion: Common maintenance guidance limits trim tab free play to approximately 2% or 1% depending on the relative tab size.

pregunta: Before removing a hydraulic pressure regulator, maintenance personnel should first:
opciones: Relieve system pressure and cap the lines
explicacion: Hydraulic pressure should always be relieved and open lines capped before removing a pressure regulator.

pregunta: A properly swaged aircraft cable terminal is rated at approximately:
explicacion: A correctly installed swaged terminal develops essentially the full rated strength of the cable.

pregunta: When is an aircraft pitot-static system leak check normally required?
opciones: Every 24 months, and whenever the system has been opened or an instrument replaced
Only after replacing the pitot tube
Only when the altimeter is inaccurate
explicacion: Regulations generally require pitot-static system testing every 24 months for IFR operation, and after maintenance affecting the system. A typical static leak test allows no more than 100 ft/min leakage.

pregunta: During a pitot system leak test at 150 knots, the allowable leakage after one minute should not exceed:
explicacion: A common maintenance specification allows a maximum loss of 10 knots during a one-minute pitot leak test at 150 knots. Always refer to the applicable maintenance manual.

pregunta: How is the electrical load of an aircraft determined?
opciones: By comparing the total electrical loads to the maximum generator output
By adding only the AC electrical loads
explicacion: Electrical load analysis is performed by summing all aircraft electrical loads and comparing them with the maximum generator or alternator output.

pregunta: What operating principle is used by most aircraft autopilot systems?
explicacion: Most aircraft autopilot systems operate as closed-loop systems, continuously sensing aircraft attitude or flight path and correcting deviations.

pregunta: In an air-cycle air conditioning system, where does the major pressure and temperature drop occur?
explicacion: The expansion turbine extracts energy from the compressed air, producing the greatest pressure and temperature drop.

pregunta: The general formula for wing aspect ratio is:
explicacion: The general aerodynamic formula for aspect ratio is Wing Span² divided by Wing Area.

pregunta: Vortex generators are small aerodynamic surfaces installed on a wing primarily to:
opciones: Delay airflow separation by energizing the boundary layer
explicacion: Vortex generators create small vortices that energize the boundary layer, delaying flow separation and improving low-speed control effectiveness.

pregunta: What is the primary function of the cabin outflow valve?
opciones: Control bleed air entering the cabin
Regulate cabin pressure by controlling the amount of air leaving the cabin
explicacion: The outflow valve regulates cabin pressure by controlling the rate at which cabin air is discharged overboard, either automatically or manually.

pregunta: What is the adiabatic lapse rate?
opciones: The rate at which atmospheric pressure decreases with altitude
The increase or decrease in air temperature caused by a change in altitude without heat being added or removed
The rate at which humidity changes with altitude
The rate at which wind speed changes with altitude
explicacion: An adiabatic lapse rate is the change in air temperature resulting solely from a change in altitude, with no heat added to or removed from the air.

pregunta: During a compass swing, how are compass corrections normally applied?
opciones: Correct North and South completely, East and West halfway
Correct North and East completely, then South and West halfway
Correct East and West completely only
explicacion: During a compass swing, North and East headings are normally corrected completely. South and West headings are then adjusted approximately halfway to minimize overall compass deviation.

pregunta: A stall warning system is generally designed to activate:
opciones: Approximately 10 mph (or about 4%) before the stall
explicacion: Many aircraft activate the stall warning approximately 5–10 knots (about 10 mph or 4–7%) before the actual stall. The exact value depends on the aircraft manufacturer.

pregunta: What is a cantilever wing?
opciones: A wing supported by external struts
A wing supported by flying wires
A wing with no external external bracing, supported entirely by its internal structure
explicacion: A cantilever wing is supported entirely by its internal structure and requires no external struts or bracing wires.

pregunta: What is decalage?
opciones: The angle between the wing chord and the relative wind
The difference in angle of incidence between two lifting surfaces
The twist built into a wing
explicacion: Decalage is the angular difference between the angle of incidence of two lifting surfaces, such as the wings of a biplane or the wing and horizontal stabilizer.

pregunta: What is the camber of an airfoil?
opciones: The angle between the chord line and the relative wind
The curvature of the mean camber line
explicacion: Camber is the curvature of the mean camber line of an airfoil.

pregunta: What is positive stagger on a biplane?
opciones: The lower wing is ahead of the upper wing
The upper wing is ahead of the lower wing
Both wings have the same leading edge position
The wings have different dihedral angles
explicacion: Positive stagger exists when the upper wing is positioned forward of the lower wing.

pregunta: If a propeller rotates clockwise when viewed from the cockpit, torque reaction tends to roll the aircraft:
explicacion: Torque reaction tends to roll the aircraft opposite the direction of propeller rotation. The exact tendency depends on the aircraft configuration and reference viewpoint.

pregunta: Where are leading-edge slots located?
explicacion: Leading-edge slots are installed at the leading edge of the wing to delay stall and improve low-speed handling.

pregunta: What is washout?
opciones: Greater angle of incidence at the wing tip than the wing root
Greater angle of incidence at the wing root than the wing tip
explicacion: Washout means the wing root has a greater angle of incidence than the tip. Its primary purpose is to ensure the wing root stalls before the tip, improving controllability.

pregunta: What is the purpose of control surface snubbers?
opciones: Hydraulically cushion control surface movement on the ground
explicacion: Snubbers dampen control surface movement while the aircraft is on the ground, reducing damage from wind gusts.

pregunta: What is burbling?
opciones: Breakdown of smooth airflow producing turbulence
explicacion: Burbling is the turbulent airflow produced when smooth airflow separates from the aircraft surface.

pregunta: What is the primary advantage of a Fowler flap compared with a split flap?
opciones: Increases wing area and produces more lift
explicacion: A Fowler flap moves rearward as it extends, increasing both wing area and camber, producing greater lift than a split flap.

pregunta: What is a common result of excessively over-tensioned flight control cables?
opciones: Excessive wear of pulleys and fairleads
explicacion: Excessive cable tension increases friction and causes accelerated wear of pulleys and fairleads.

pregunta: What is the primary purpose of vortex generators?
opciones: Energize the boundary layer to delay airflow separation
explicacion: Vortex generators energize the boundary layer, delaying flow separation and improving low-speed handling and control effectiveness.

pregunta: A properly installed wrapped-and-soldered cable terminal should retain approximately:
explicacion: Traditional wrapped-and-soldered terminals are generally rated at about 90% of the cable's strength, whereas properly swaged terminals retain essentially 100%.

pregunta: What is the minimum cable size on which a five-tuck hand splice may normally be used?
explicacion: Traditional aircraft cable maintenance references state that a five-tuck hand splice is normally used only on cables 3/32 inch diameter or larger. Always verify with the applicable maintenance manual.

pregunta: What is a Warren truss fuselage?
opciones: A welded tubular steel structure carrying tension and compression loads
explicacion: A Warren truss fuselage consists of welded steel tubing arranged in triangular members that carry tension and compression loads.

pregunta: What is tare weight?
opciones: The empty weight of the aircraft
The weight of equipment used while weighing the aircraft
explicacion: Tare is the weight of equipment such as chocks, jacks, or scales that must be deducted from the measured weight.

pregunta: Aircraft center of gravity limits are commonly expressed as:
opciones: Percentage of MAC or distance from the datum
explicacion: CG limits are commonly expressed as either inches from the datum or percentage of the Mean Aerodynamic Chord (MAC).

pregunta: When using a torque wrench with a crowfoot adapter in line with the wrench, which formula is used to determine the indicated torque?
explicacion: When the adapter is inline, the indicated torque must be reduced using: Tw = Ta × L / (L + A), where Ta is the desired torque, L is wrench length, and A is adapter length.

pregunta: Grade A aircraft fabric normally has approximately how many threads per inch?
explicacion: Traditional Grade A cotton aircraft fabric is commonly specified as having approximately 85 to 90 threads per inch. Values may vary with manufacturer specifications.

pregunta: A sewing machine used for aircraft fabric should normally produce approximately:
explicacion: Aircraft fabric repair references commonly specify 8 to 10 stitches per inch for machine stitching. Always follow the manufacturer's repair manual.

pregunta: A hand-sewn aircraft fabric seam should normally have at least:
explicacion: Traditional aircraft fabric hand stitching typically requires a minimum of 4 stitches per inch.

pregunta: During a baseball stitch repair on aircraft fabric, the stitches should normally be locked:
opciones: Only at the end of the repair
explicacion: Traditional aircraft fabric repair practices recommend locking the baseball stitch approximately every 8 to 10 stitches.

pregunta: Blushing in a dope finish is most commonly caused by:
explicacion: Blushing is the milky appearance caused by moisture condensing in the dope during rapid evaporation under humid conditions.

pregunta: Rib-lacing knots should normally be located on the:
explicacion: Standard rib-lacing procedures place the knots on the lower surface to reduce aerodynamic disturbance.

pregunta: Tape applied over a sewn aircraft fabric repair should overlap the repair by at least:
explicacion: Traditional aircraft fabric repair practices specify approximately 1½ inches minimum overlap over sewn repairs.

pregunta: Tape applied over the trailing edge of a fabric-covered wing should normally be notched every:
explicacion: Trailing-edge tape is commonly notched every 6 inches to reduce the possibility of the entire strip loosening.

pregunta: The warp threads of aircraft fabric should normally be installed:
opciones: Perpendicular to the line of flight
Parallel to the line of flight
At 45 degrees to the line of flight
explicacion: Warp threads are installed parallel to the line of flight because they are the strongest direction of the fabric.

pregunta: Aircraft dope should ideally be applied under what environmental conditions?
opciones: Approximately 70°F and less than 65% relative humidity
explicacion: Traditional aircraft fabric practices recommend applying dope at approximately 70°F with relative humidity below 65%. Manufacturer recommendations always take precedence.

pregunta: Pinholes in a dope finish are most commonly caused by:
explicacion: Pinholes usually result from trapped solvents caused by insufficient drying time between successive coats.

pregunta: What is bias in aircraft fabric?
opciones: The strongest direction of the fabric
A cut made diagonally across the warp and fill threads
explicacion: Bias refers to a cut made diagonally across the weave, providing greater flexibility around curved surfaces.

pregunta: Orange peel in a sprayed dope finish is commonly caused by:
opciones: Dope too thick, excessive spray distance, or very dry air
explicacion: Orange peel commonly results from overly thick dope, spraying too far from the surface, or environmental conditions causing poor flow-out.

pregunta: Stress corrosion cracking in aluminum alloy fittings is most commonly associated with:
explicacion: Stress corrosion cracking develops when susceptible aluminum alloys are subjected to sustained tensile stress in a corrosive environment.

pregunta: Are automatic-reset circuit breakers normally used on aircraft?
opciones: No, manual-reset circuit breakers are normally used
explicacion: Most certified aircraft use manual-reset circuit breakers so that repeated faults cannot automatically re-energize a failed circuit. Some aircraft may incorporate automatic-reset devices for specific applications.

pregunta: What is camber in landing gear alignment?
opciones: The angle of the wheel in relation to the aircraft's vertical axis
The caster angle of the nose wheel
explicacion: Camber is the inward or outward tilt of the wheel when viewed from the front of the aircraft.

pregunta: When rigging the rudder and rudder pedals, the rudder should be aligned with the:
explicacion: With the rudder pedals neutral, the rudder should normally be aligned with the aircraft centerline unless otherwise specified by the manufacturer.

pregunta: What is the purpose of a Flettner tab?
opciones: Reduce pilot control forces by assisting control surface movement
Lock the control surface on the ground
explicacion: A Flettner tab is a type of servo tab that assists movement of the primary control surface, reducing pilot effort.

pregunta: If one pneumatic de-ice boot fails to inflate while the others operate normally, the most likely cause is:
opciones: A leak in the affected boot or its supply line
explicacion: A leak in the individual boot or its pneumatic plumbing is the most common reason a single de-ice boot fails to inflate.

pregunta: Aircraft control locks should normally have:
opciones: A red warning streamer or ribbon
explicacion: Control locks should have a highly visible warning streamer to remind personnel that the controls are locked before flight.

pregunta: What is the purpose of a wing flap relief valve?
opciones: Prevent flap movement at excessive airspeeds
explicacion: A flap relief valve protects the flap system by preventing excessive aerodynamic loads at high airspeeds.

pregunta: Can the dihedral angle normally be adjusted on a certificated aircraft?
opciones: No, unless specifically authorized by the manufacturer
Yes, by adjusting the wing struts
explicacion: Dihedral is established by the aircraft structure and is normally not adjustable unless specified in approved maintenance procedures.

pregunta: Can scratches be removed from aircraft acrylic (Plexiglas) windows?
opciones: No, scratches can never be removed
Minor scratches can often be polished out using approved materials
Only by sanding with coarse sandpaper
explicacion: Minor scratches in acrylic transparencies can often be removed with approved polishing compounds. Deep cracks or severe damage require replacement.

pregunta: When servicing an oleo strut, the correct procedure is to:
opciones: Add nitrogen first, then hydraulic fluid
Completely deflate the strut, compress it, service the fluid, then recharge with nitrogen or air as specified
explicacion: Standard servicing requires relieving the gas pressure, compressing the strut, servicing the hydraulic fluid, and then recharging with the specified gas.

pregunta: If brake clearances are adjusted too small, the most likely result is:
explicacion: Insufficient brake clearance causes the brakes to drag, resulting in overheating and accelerated wear.

pregunta: Aircraft tires should normally be stored:
opciones: In a cool, dry place in a vertical position
explicacion: Aircraft tires should be stored vertically in a cool, dry area away from sunlight, ozone sources, and petroleum products.

pregunta: The maximum charging voltage for a typical 12-volt (6-cell) lead-acid aircraft battery is approximately:
explicacion: Approximately 14.1 volts is a common charging voltage for many 12-volt lead-acid batteries. Always follow the battery manufacturer's maintenance instructions.

pregunta: Semi-monocoque aircraft structures primarily use:
opciones: Longerons, frames, bulkheads, and stressed skin
explicacion: Semi-monocoque construction distributes loads through the stressed skin together with longerons, frames, and bulkheads.

pregunta: The basic sensing elements of most aircraft autopilot systems are:
explicacion: Most autopilot systems use gyroscopes and other sensing elements to detect aircraft attitude and deviations from the selected flight path.

pregunta: How can the pilot normally override an engaged autopilot?
opciones: Through the control wheel or flight controller override
By selecting the landing gear down
explicacion: Most autopilot systems allow manual override through the control wheel or by disconnecting the autopilot using the flight controls or disconnect switch.

pregunta: Wing Station Zero (WS 0) is normally measured from the:
explicacion: Wing stations are referenced to the aircraft buttock line for lateral location measurements.

pregunta: Aircraft control cables should generally be replaced when wear reaches approximately:
explicacion: Some training references use 40–50% wear as a replacement guideline, but actual replacement criteria are specified by the aircraft manufacturer and cable construction.

pregunta: The ability of an aircraft to be directed along a selected flight path is known as:
explicacion: Maneuverability is the ability of an aircraft to be directed along a desired flight path.

pregunta: One advantage of an integral fuel tank is that it:
opciones: Reduces structural weight by using the wing structure as the fuel tank
Eliminates the need for boost pumps
explicacion: Integral (wet-wing) fuel tanks reduce weight because the aircraft structure itself forms the fuel tank.

pregunta: After installing a new aircraft tire, the inflation pressure should normally be rechecked after:
explicacion: A newly installed tire is commonly rechecked after 12–24 hours because the pressure may stabilize after installation.

pregunta: Small heat-check cracks on an aircraft brake drum are:
explicacion: Allowable brake drum cracks depend on the manufacturer's maintenance limits. Always consult the applicable maintenance manual.

pregunta: A DC generator uses what type of commutator?
explicacion: A DC generator uses a split-ring commutator to mechanically rectify the generated alternating current into direct current.

pregunta: Which combination contributes most to aircraft stability?
opciones: Dihedral for lateral stability and longitudinal tail arrangement for longitudinal stability
explicacion: Dihedral improves lateral stability, while the horizontal stabilizer and longitudinal configuration provide longitudinal stability.

pregunta: Internal leakage in a brake master cylinder will most likely result in:
opciones: Brake fading or inability to maintain pressure
explicacion: Internal leakage allows hydraulic fluid to bypass the piston seal, reducing braking effectiveness.

pregunta: The primary purpose of a hydraulic accumulator is to:
opciones: Store hydraulic energy and assist the pump during periods of high demand
Increase system pressure above relief-valve setting
explicacion: An accumulator stores hydraulic energy, dampens pressure fluctuations, and supplies additional fluid during peak demand.

pregunta: What is the primary purpose of a de-booster valve in a brake system?
opciones: Reduce brake pressure for smoother application and release
explicacion: A de-booster valve reduces hydraulic pressure reaching the brakes, providing smoother brake application.

pregunta: An Integrated Drive Generator (IDG) combines a:
explicacion: An IDG combines a brushless AC generator with a Constant Speed Drive (CSD) to provide constant-frequency electrical power.

pregunta: Phosphate ester hydraulic fluid systems normally use seals made of:
explicacion: Butyl rubber is commonly used because it is compatible with phosphate ester hydraulic fluids such as Skydrol.

pregunta: ADF sense antennas are normally mounted:
opciones: On top of the vertical stabilizer
On the bottom of the aircraft as far forward as practical
explicacion: ADF antennas are commonly installed on the underside of the aircraft, as far forward as practical, to minimize interference.

pregunta: Selecting EMERGENCY or 100% on a diluter-demand oxygen regulator provides:
explicacion: Selecting 100% or EMERGENCY bypasses the normal air dilution and supplies pure oxygen.

pregunta: During isobaric operation, the cabin pressure controller primarily senses:
explicacion: The controller senses cabin pressure and modulates the outflow valve to maintain the selected cabin altitude.

pregunta: A VOR antenna is most commonly installed:
opciones: On top of the vertical stabilizer
On the upper fuselage or vertical stabilizer
Inside the nose landing gear bay
explicacion: VOR antennas are commonly mounted on the top of the fuselage or on the vertical stabilizer to provide good omnidirectional reception.

pregunta: The dihedral angle of a wing is commonly measured at the:
opciones: Front spar or specified reference point
explicacion: The manufacturer specifies the reference location for measuring dihedral, commonly at or near the front spar.

pregunta: The neutral position of a trim tab is generally:
opciones: Flush with the control surface only
Approximately 1° to 2° from the streamlined position
explicacion: A trim tab is generally rigged approximately 1° to 2° from the streamlined position unless otherwise specified by the manufacturer.

pregunta: Before servicing or removing a hydraulic accumulator, maintenance personnel should first:
opciones: Relieve all hydraulic pressure and remove the gas preload
explicacion: The accumulator gas charge must be safely released and hydraulic pressure relieved before servicing to prevent injury.

pregunta: Most aircraft autopilot sensing systems operate on the principle of:
opciones: Relative motion between a gyro and its supporting structure
explicacion: Autopilot sensing units use gyroscopic principles, detecting relative motion between the gyro and its supporting structure.

pregunta: The primary sensing instruments used by most autopilot systems are:
opciones: Gyroscopes such as attitude and directional gyros
explicacion: Autopilot systems commonly receive attitude and heading information from gyroscopic instruments.

pregunta: The primary purpose of performing a compass swing is to:
opciones: Calibrate the magnetic compass and determine deviation
explicacion: A compass swing is performed to determine and correct compass deviation caused by magnetic influences in the aircraft.

pregunta: The primary function of a hydraulic pump is to:
opciones: Move or circulate hydraulic fluid through the system
explicacion: A hydraulic pump creates flow. Pressure is produced only when that flow encounters resistance.

pregunta: On a vibrator-type voltage regulator, system voltage is adjusted by:
opciones: Adjusting the spring tension on the regulator contact points
explicacion: Mechanical voltage regulators are adjusted by changing the spring tension acting on the voltage regulating contacts.

pregunta: To protect generator rectifier diodes from voltage surges, maintenance personnel should:
opciones: Disconnect the battery while the engine is running
Keep the battery connected whenever the generator is operating
explicacion: Disconnecting the battery while the generator or alternator is operating may create damaging voltage spikes.

pregunta: Electrolyte spilled from a nickel-cadmium (Ni-Cd) battery is commonly neutralized with:
explicacion: Potassium hydroxide electrolyte from Ni-Cd batteries is neutralized using a boric acid solution.

pregunta: The state of charge of a lead-acid battery is normally determined using a:
explicacion: A hydrometer measures the electrolyte specific gravity, which indicates the state of charge of a lead-acid battery.

pregunta: If no manufacturer limits are available, a satisfactory differential compression test result is generally considered to be:
explicacion: A commonly accepted training value is 80/60, although the engine manufacturer's limits always take precedence.

pregunta: A complete Instrument Landing System (ILS) consists primarily of:
opciones: Localizer, Glide Slope and Marker Beacons
explicacion: The primary ILS components are the Localizer, Glide Slope and Marker Beacons.

pregunta: The localizer portion of an Instrument Landing System provides:
opciones: Lateral guidance for runway centerline alignment
explicacion: The localizer provides left/right guidance to align the aircraft with the runway centerline.

pregunta: A battery capable of supplying 7 amperes continuously for 5 hours has a capacity of:
explicacion: Battery capacity is calculated by multiplying current by time: 7 A × 5 h = 35 Ah.

pregunta: The contents of most portable aircraft fire extinguishers are checked by:
opciones: Checking the color of the agent
explicacion: Many portable aircraft fire extinguishers are serviced by comparing their actual weight with the manufacturer's specified weight.

pregunta: Dissymmetry of lift in a helicopter is primarily caused by:
opciones: The advancing blade producing more lift than the retreating blade
explicacion: As the helicopter moves forward, the advancing blade experiences a higher relative wind than the retreating blade, producing dissymmetry of lift.

pregunta: Gyroscopic precession causes rotor blade movement to occur:
opciones: Immediately at the point of control input
Approximately 90° later in the direction of rotation
explicacion: Rotor systems respond approximately 90° later in the direction of rotation due to gyroscopic precession.

pregunta: The primary purpose of rotor blade tracking is to:
opciones: Ensure all blades follow the same tip-path plane
explicacion: Blade tracking ensures every rotor blade follows the same tip-path plane, reducing vibration.

pregunta: Blade twist is incorporated into helicopter rotor blades primarily to:
opciones: Improve lift distribution along the blade span
explicacion: Blade twist compensates for the increase in rotational speed toward the blade tip, improving lift distribution.

pregunta: The coning angle of a helicopter rotor is primarily determined by the relationship between:
explicacion: Coning angle increases as lift overcomes centrifugal force acting on the rotor blades.

pregunta: The primary loads acting on a helicopter rotor mast are:
explicacion: The rotor mast primarily carries tensile and torsional loads transmitted by the rotor system.

pregunta: If the overrunning clutch (freewheeling unit) seizes, the result is that the:
opciones: Main rotor can no longer autorotate
Engine becomes disconnected from the transmission
explicacion: A seized overrunning clutch prevents the rotor from freewheeling during autorotation.

pregunta: A hard rotor damper generally results in:
opciones: A slower but more stable response
explicacion: A hard damper produces quicker control response but can make the helicopter less stable.

pregunta: Pitch links connect the rotating swashplate to the:
explicacion: Pitch links transmit swashplate movement to each rotor blade, changing blade pitch.

pregunta: The drive link in a helicopter rotor system connects the:
opciones: Rotating swashplate to the rotor mast
Collective lever to the cyclic stick
Pitch horn to the blade grip
explicacion: The drive link transmits rotation from the mast to the rotating swashplate.

pregunta: The rotor brake is commonly installed:
opciones: Between the engine and the main transmission
explicacion: Most rotor brakes are installed on the transmission input or power shaft between the engine and transmission.

pregunta: Artificial feel in a hydraulically boosted helicopter control system is normally provided by a:
explicacion: A gradient unit provides artificial control feel when hydraulic boost removes normal aerodynamic feedback.

pregunta: Most turbine helicopter fuel systems are:
explicacion: Most turbine-powered helicopters use forced-feed fuel systems to ensure continuous fuel delivery.

pregunta: Vertical flight of a helicopter is primarily controlled by:
explicacion: Changing collective pitch changes lift equally on all rotor blades, controlling vertical movement.

pregunta: According to Newton's Third Law, helicopter torque reaction causes the fuselage to:
opciones: Rotate opposite the direction of main rotor rotation
Rotate in the same direction as the rotor
explicacion: The fuselage tends to rotate opposite the direction of main rotor rotation unless counteracted by the tail rotor or another anti-torque system.

pregunta: Blade alignment is performed to establish the correct relationship of the blade center of gravity to the:
explicacion: Blade alignment establishes the proper chordwise balance relationship of the rotor blade to minimize vibration during operation.

pregunta: A helicopter rotor system that is out of track will normally produce:
explicacion: An out-of-track rotor typically produces a 1-per-revolution (1:1) vertical vibration.

pregunta: In normal flight, the main rotor mast primarily absorbs:
explicacion: The rotor mast transmits both torsional loads from rotor torque and tensile loads generated by rotor lift.

pregunta: The rotating portion of the swashplate is driven by a drive link attached to the:
explicacion: The rotating swashplate is driven from the rotor mast through a drive link.

pregunta: On a free-turbine helicopter, the collective twist grip primarily controls:
explicacion: The twist grip is used to control compressor (gas producer) RPM on helicopters equipped with free-turbine engines.

pregunta: A movable horizontal stabilizer on a helicopter is primarily used to:
opciones: Increase controllability and extend the center-of-gravity range
explicacion: Movable horizontal stabilizers improve longitudinal controllability and allow a wider center-of-gravity operating range.

pregunta: On most turbine-powered helicopters, the rotor brake is normally operated by:
explicacion: Most turbine helicopters use a dedicated hydraulic system to operate the rotor brake.

pregunta: Helicopter torque reaction causes the fuselage to rotate:
opciones: In the same direction as the main rotor
Opposite the direction of main rotor rotation
explicacion: According to Newton's Third Law, the fuselage tends to rotate opposite the direction of the main rotor.

pregunta: A dual tachometer in a helicopter normally indicates:
opciones: Engine RPM and main rotor RPM
Main rotor RPM and tail rotor RPM
explicacion: The dual tachometer allows the pilot to monitor both engine RPM and main rotor RPM simultaneously.

pregunta: On many metal helicopter rotor blades, the skin primarily:
opciones: Acts as the main structural spar
Serves as a wrap-around skin providing the aerodynamic shape
explicacion: Many metal rotor blades use a wrap-around skin to form the aerodynamic contour, while the spar carries the primary structural loads.

pregunta: Conical elastomeric bearings used in helicopter rotor systems are designed to:
opciones: Absorb high axial and radial loads while allowing limited movement
explicacion: Conical elastomeric bearings support high radial and axial loads while permitting the limited movement required by the rotor system.

pregunta: Aircraft hydraulic priority valves are normally:
explicacion: Priority valves are normally hydraulically actuated to ensure hydraulic pressure is supplied first to essential systems.

pregunta: The variation in blade angle between the rotor root and tip is known as the:
explicacion: Blade twist (twist angle) is the gradual change in blade incidence from root to tip to provide a more uniform lift distribution.

pregunta: Rotor blade tracking is performed to ensure that all rotor blades:
opciones: Have the same pitch angle at all times
Follow the same tip-path plane during rotation
explicacion: Blade tracking ensures that every blade follows the same tip-path plane, minimizing vibration and improving rotor smoothness.

pregunta: During inspection of an aircraft terminal strip, the terminal studs should be:
explicacion: Terminal strip studs must be secured against rotation to prevent damage to wiring and electrical connections.

pregunta: A Pratt truss fuselage is characterized by:
opciones: Welded steel tubes with diagonal members only
Vertical members between longerons reinforced with wire bracing
explicacion: A Pratt truss uses vertical members between the longerons with diagonal wire bracing to carry structural loads.

pregunta: On aircraft that use AC generators but no DC generators, the batteries are normally charged by:
explicacion: Transformer Rectifier Units (TRUs) convert AC power into DC power to charge the aircraft batteries and supply DC buses.

pregunta: In a vapor-cycle air conditioning system, heat absorbed by the refrigerant is rejected at the:
explicacion: The condenser rejects heat absorbed by the refrigerant to the outside air.

pregunta: What should be used to clean an aircraft tire bead before installation?
explicacion: Aircraft tire beads are normally cleaned with isopropyl alcohol before installation.

pregunta: An automatic continuous-flow oxygen regulator will:
opciones: Automatically adjust oxygen flow as altitude changes
Require manual adjustment after every 1,000 feet
explicacion: Automatic continuous-flow regulators vary oxygen flow automatically as cabin altitude changes.

pregunta: A manual continuous-flow oxygen regulator requires the user to:
opciones: Reset the oxygen flow as altitude changes
Switch between AC and DC power
explicacion: Manual continuous-flow regulators require periodic adjustment to match changing altitude.

pregunta: The purpose of a negative pressure relief valve is to:
opciones: Prevent outside pressure from becoming greater than cabin pressure
explicacion: A negative pressure relief valve protects the fuselage by preventing excessive negative differential pressure.

pregunta: What is the maximum number of terminal lugs normally permitted on a single terminal strip stud?
explicacion: Standard aircraft practices generally limit terminal strip studs to four terminal lugs unless otherwise specified.

pregunta: Aircraft fuses are rated according to:
explicacion: Aircraft fuses are rated by the amount of current, in amperes, they can safely carry.

pregunta: Air is normally removed from an aircraft hydraulic system by:
opciones: Operating the hydraulic components through several complete cycles
explicacion: Cycling the hydraulic components repeatedly allows trapped air to return to the reservoir and be removed.

pregunta: A leak in the aircraft static system is normally detected by observing the:
opciones: Altimeter after applying the specified test altitude
explicacion: Static system leakage is checked by monitoring the altimeter for allowable altitude loss after a specified test pressure is applied.

pregunta: When using a cable tensiometer, the correct ______ must be installed for the cable being checked.
explicacion: Cable tensiometers require the proper riser for the cable diameter to obtain an accurate reading.

pregunta: The specified tire inflation pressure for an aircraft wheel is found in the:
explicacion: Aircraft tire servicing pressures are specified in the Aircraft Maintenance Manual (AMM).

pregunta: The two most common types of AC motors used in aircraft are:
explicacion: Induction and synchronous motors are the most commonly used AC motors in aircraft systems.

pregunta: A common method of restoring residual magnetism in a DC generator field is to:
explicacion: Residual magnetism can be restored by flashing the generator field using the approved maintenance procedure.

pregunta: Excessive arcing at generator brushes is commonly corrected by adjusting the:
explicacion: Proper brush spring tension helps maintain good brush contact and minimizes arcing.

pregunta: Internal leakage in a brake master cylinder will usually cause the brake pedal to:
opciones: Slowly sink while pressure is maintained
explicacion: Internal seal leakage allows fluid to bypass the piston, causing the brake pedal to slowly creep downward under constant pressure.

pregunta: An aircraft with good longitudinal stability has little tendency to:
explicacion: Longitudinal stability refers to the aircraft's tendency to resist pitching disturbances.

pregunta: The aspect ratio of a wing is correctly calculated as:
explicacion: Aspect ratio is calculated by dividing the square of the wingspan by the wing area.

pregunta: Which helicopter rotor head design is least able to compensate for dissymmetry of lift?
explicacion: A rigid rotor head has no flapping hinges and therefore has the least inherent ability to compensate for dissymmetry of lift compared with articulated or semi-rigid designs. :contentReference[oaicite:0]{index=0}

pregunta: Which rotor head design best compensates for the Coriolis effect?
explicacion: A fully articulated rotor head uses lead-lag hinges that allow the blades to compensate for Coriolis effect during flight. :contentReference[oaicite:1]{index=1}

pregunta: A movable horizontal stabilizer on a helicopter is normally actuated by the:
explicacion: On helicopters equipped with a movable horizontal stabilizer, the stabilizer is normally positioned as a function of cyclic control inputs or associated control linkage. :contentReference[oaicite:2]{index=2}

pregunta: A helicopter transmission is normally lubricated with:
explicacion: Most helicopter transmissions use their own dedicated lubricant rather than sharing engine oil. :contentReference[oaicite:3]{index=3}

pregunta: The horizontal stabilizer used on many helicopters typically has what airfoil shape?
explicacion: Many helicopter horizontal stabilizers use an inverted asymmetrical airfoil to improve longitudinal stability in forward flight. :contentReference[oaicite:4]{index=4}

pregunta: In steady forward flight, a helicopter changes direction primarily by:
opciones: Applying positive tail rotor thrust only
explicacion: Directional changes in forward flight are accomplished primarily by tilting the rotor disc with cyclic control, which changes the direction of the total rotor thrust. :contentReference[oaicite:5]{index=5}

pregunta: If the amplitude of a helicopter vibration remains constant throughout the entire rotor RPM range, the rotor is most likely:
explicacion: A vibration whose amplitude remains essentially constant throughout the rotor RPM range is characteristic of a chordwise imbalance. An out-of-track condition normally produces a 1-per-revolution vertical vibration rather than a constant-amplitude vibration. :contentReference[oaicite:1]{index=1}

pregunta: For wire selection, an intermittent electrical load is defined as a load that operates for a maximum of:
explicacion: For wire sizing purposes, an intermittent load is one that operates for a maximum of 2 minutes. :contentReference[oaicite:0]{index=0}

pregunta: Before installing an aircraft wheel, the wheel contact surfaces should be cleaned with:
explicacion: Wheel contact surfaces should be cleaned with isopropyl alcohol before installation. :contentReference[oaicite:1]{index=1}

pregunta: An ADF radio system consists of:
opciones: Receiver, VOR antenna, DME antenna and indicator
Receiver, loop antenna, sense antenna, RMI (or indicator), and control panel
Receiver, glide slope antenna, localizer antenna and encoder
Receiver, transponder, encoder and control head
explicacion: A complete ADF installation includes a receiver, loop antenna, sense antenna, indicator/RMI and a control panel. :contentReference[oaicite:2]{index=2}

pregunta: A Mode C transponder system includes:
opciones: Control head and glide slope antenna
explicacion: Mode C operation requires an encoding altimeter (encoder) along with the transponder function selector. :contentReference[oaicite:3]{index=3}

pregunta: During inspection, the tire bead seat should be checked for:
opciones: Evidence of tire tool damage, rim chafing and slippage
explicacion: Inspect the bead seat area for tool damage, chafing against the rim and evidence of slippage. :contentReference[oaicite:4]{index=4}

pregunta: The refrigerant traditionally used in aircraft vapor-cycle air conditioning systems is:
explicacion: Older aircraft vapor-cycle systems commonly used dichlorodifluoromethane (R-12) refrigerant. :contentReference[oaicite:5]{index=5}

pregunta: An aircraft that responds rapidly and accurately to pilot control inputs is said to have good:
explicacion: Controllability is the characteristic that describes how readily an aircraft responds to pilot control inputs. :contentReference[oaicite:6]{index=6}

pregunta: The thickness of the boundary layer is primarily affected by:
opciones: The surface roughness of the wing
explicacion: Surface roughness has a significant effect on boundary-layer development and transition. :contentReference[oaicite:7]{index=7}

pregunta: The upper surface of an airfoil is called the:
explicacion: The upper surface of an airfoil is known as the extrados. :contentReference[oaicite:8]{index=8}

pregunta: Cabin pressurization systems normally operate in which basic modes?
explicacion: Aircraft pressurization systems generally operate in an isobaric range followed by a constant differential pressure range. :contentReference[oaicite:9]{index=9}

pregunta: The air used by a Freon vapor-cycle condenser is normally:
explicacion: The condenser of a vapor-cycle system is cooled by outside ambient air. :contentReference[oaicite:10]{index=10}

pregunta: What would be the effect if the piston return spring broke in a brake master cylinder?
opciones: The brake pedal would lock in the up position
explicacion: A broken piston return spring prevents the piston from fully returning, causing the compensating port to remain closed and the brake to drag.

pregunta: What component in a vapor-cycle air conditioning system would most likely be at fault if the system would not take a Freon charge?
explicacion: A restricted or faulty expansion valve can prevent the system from accepting a refrigerant charge.

pregunta: What controls the operation of the cabin pressure regulator?
explicacion: The cabin pressure regulator operates by sensing and responding to cabin air pressure.

pregunta: The basic air-cycle cooling system consists of:
opciones: A source of compressed air, heat exchangers, and an expansion turbine
A compressor, receiver-drier, and expansion valve
explicacion: A basic air-cycle cooling system uses compressed air, heat exchangers, and an expansion turbine to produce cooling.

pregunta: What is the purpose of evacuating a vapor-cycle (Freon) air conditioning system before charging?
opciones: To remove moisture from the system
explicacion: Evacuating the system removes moisture and non-condensable gases before charging with refrigerant.

pregunta: Chattering of the hydraulic pump during engine run-up is usually an indication of:
opciones: An air leak in the pump inlet line
explicacion: Air entering the pump inlet causes cavitation, producing a characteristic chattering noise.
